titleStmt.title: Introduction to Damaged Stele from Pingyin County

div.head: 平陰縣殘碑
div.p: 這一通小石碑殘塊僅30釐米高，上部刻有一個蟠龍環繞的佛龕。僅有拓片傳世。柳文金 無頁碼 此碑曾由柳文金收藏，現佚失或藏主不明。該碑原址不詳。根據柳文金，此碑來自濟寧縣，但一般認為原出或存平陰縣。張總 4；賴非169。 從書法風格上來看，它和兗州金口壩出土的石碑很接近，其中一通碑的紀年為河清三年（564）。賴非 169。
碑上所刻的佛經節文出自《思益梵天所問經》，《大正藏》T#586, 15: 33-62。是對菩薩六波羅蜜的定義。相同的經文段落也出現在鄒城已毀的的尖山12號中。見《中國佛教石經·山東省》第二卷。
除了這兩處銘文，關於菩薩六波羅蜜的經文在山東省現存還有兩處：洪頂山2號和鄒城鐵山1號，同上。這兩處的六波羅蜜引自《大方等大集經》。《大方等大集經》關於六波羅蜜的定義是直截了當的，而《思益梵天所問經》卻用似非而是的方式闡明六波羅蜜， 提出了一般所認為的六個完美性質──即佈施、戒律、忍辱、精進、禪定和智慧──的各種對立面。

div.head: Damaged Stele from Pingyin County
div.p:
The fragment of a small stele, with intertwining dragons enclosing a Buddha niche in the upper part, measures only about 30 cm in height. Only a rubbing is known. unpaginated
The stele was formerly in the collection of Liu Wenjin. It is now either lost or held in an unknown private collection. Its provenance is unclear. According to Liu Wenjin, it came from Jining County; yet it has been assumed that it was found or preserved in Pingyin County. 4; 169. Stylistically, it is said to be close to other stelae found at the Jin­kou Embankment 金口壩 in Yanzhou 兗州. One of these comparable stelae dates to the third year of the heqing era (564). 169.

The carved text passage gives a definition of the Six Perfections of the bodhisattva. It is taken from the Sutra of the Questions of Viśeṣacintibrahma
( Siyi fantian suo wen jing
思益梵天所問經).T#586, 15: 33-62. The same text was carved as the now demolished inscription JS 12 at Mount Jian 尖山 in Zoucheng 鄒城 (see Shandong, vol. 2).

In addition to these two inscriptions, text passages about the Six Perfections of a bodhisattva have survived at two other sites in Shandong Province: In inscription HDS 2 at Mount Hongding 洪頂山 and inscription Tie 1 at Mount Tie 鐵山 in Zoucheng (see Shandong, vol. 2), the Six Perfections are quoted from the Vast and Universal Great Collection Sutra ( *Mahāvaipulyamahāsaṃnipāta-sūtra,
Da­feng­deng daji jing 大方等大集經). In contrast to the rather straight forward definitions of the Six Perfections taken from the Great Collection Sutra , the text from the Sutra of the Questions of Viśeṣacinti­brahma
elucidates the Six Perfections in paradoxes, naming the opposites of what are commonly held to be the perfections: generosity, discipline, patience, vigorous effort, meditation, and wisdom.
